steps.title: Close the relative clause
steps.content: The "which takes place" clause interrupts the main clause between the subject, the novel, and the main verb, tells. The blank must close that interrupter before the verb resumes.

steps.title: Choose comma before verb
steps.content: A uses a colon where the relative clause needs closure. B creates a fragment. D makes "village" read like the subject of "tells." The remaining punctuation closes the interrupter and lets the main verb resume. The answer is C.
